content:
FINAL IVX OWNERSHIP + PRODUCTION QA — VERIFY INDEPENDENCE, DEPLOYMENT, AND LIVE FUNCTIONALITY

Rork stated that IVX Holdings is independent and that all application code and infrastructure are owned by IVX.

Do not provide another general summary.

Perform a complete evidence-based QA audit proving:

1. IVX ownership
2. No Rork runtime dependency
3. Production deployment alignment
4. Feature functionality
5. Autonomous dashboard visibility
6. Android and landing-page readiness

Do not create a new project.
Do not duplicate existing modules.
Use the current IVX Holdings workspace and production systems.

==================================================
SECTION 1 — OWNERSHIP AUDIT
==================================================

Verify ownership and administrative control for:

1. GitHub repository
2. Render backend service
3. AWS account
4. S3 bucket
5. CloudFront distribution
6. Route 53 or active DNS provider
7. Supabase project
8. Expo/EAS project
9. Android application package
10. iOS application identifier
11. Production domains
12. Chat domain
13. API domain
14. Push-notification project
15. Analytics accounts

Return:

RESOURCE | ACCOUNT OWNER | ADMIN ACCESS | RORK DEPENDENCY | PASS/FAIL | EVIDENCE

Required evidence:

- repository owner
- repository URL
- active branch
- GitHub HEAD SHA
- Render service owner
- Render service ID
- AWS account identifier
- S3 bucket name
- CloudFront distribution ID
- Supabase project reference
- Expo project ID
- Android package
- iOS bundle identifier
- DNS nameservers
- production domain records

Do not expose tokens, passwords, private keys, or secret values.

==================================================
SECTION 2 — RORK DEPENDENCY AUDIT
==================================================

Search the complete codebase for:

- rork
- rork.com
- rork SDK
- Rork API
- Rork router
- Rork runtime
- Rork auth
- Rork deployment webhook
- Rork environment variables
- Rork telemetry
- Rork storage
- Rork-hosted assets
- Rork-owned URLs
- Rork-specific package dependencies

Return every occurrence:

FILE | LINE | DEPENDENCY TYPE | REQUIRED AT RUNTIME YES/NO | ACTION

Rules:

- Development comments or historical metadata may remain only if harmless.
- No production login, deployment, API, database, media, or routing function may depend on Rork.
- No production asset may load from a Rork-controlled domain.
- No production deployment may require the Rork workspace.

If a runtime dependency exists:

1. Replace it.
2. Commit the replacement.
3. Deploy it.
4. Verify production without Rork.

==================================================
SECTION 3 — SOURCE CONTROL QA
==================================================

Verify:

- repository is owned by IVX
- main branch is protected appropriately
- latest source exists on GitHub
- no critical code exists only in the Rork workspace
- local code equals GitHub
- production backend equals GitHub
- production frontend comes from the approved GitHub release
- Android APK comes from the approved GitHub release

Return:

COMPONENT | SHA | SOURCE | DEPLOYMENT ID | MATCH YES/NO

Include:

- Local Git
- GitHub HEAD
- Backend
- Landing page
- Chat frontend
- Android APK
- iOS build, if available

A local-only commit is a failure.
A production deployment from stale GitHub is a failure.

==================================================
SECTION 4 — DISASTER RECOVERY TEST
==================================================

Prove IVX can operate without Rork.

Create a documented clean-room recovery procedure covering:

1. Clone repository from GitHub.
2. Install dependencies.
3. Configure environment variables.
4. Run backend locally.
5. Run landing page locally.
6. Run Expo app locally.
7. Build Android.
8. Build iOS.
9. Deploy backend.
10. Deploy landing page.
11. Connect Supabase.
12. Configure AWS/CDN.
13. Configure domains.
14. Restore database backup.
15. Restore storage/media.
16. Verify production.

Required deliverable:

IVX_DISASTER_RECOVERY.md

Also create:

- environment variable name inventory
- service inventory
- deployment runbook
- database backup procedure
- rollback procedure

Do not include secret values.

==================================================
SECTION 5 — BACKEND QA
==================================================

Test all live production backend routes.

At minimum:

- /health
- /readiness
- /version
- /diagnostics
- /api/reels
- /api/reels/:id
- /api/properties
- /api/properties/:id
- /api/deals
- /api/deals/:id
- /api/members/authoritative-count
- /api/metrics/authoritative-count
- /api/trpc/waitlist.getStats
- registration
- login
- owner login
- owner action list
- autonomous dashboard
- public rooms
- public messages
- landing deploy status

Return:

ROUTE | METHOD | EXPECTED | ACTUAL | CONTENT TYPE | PASS/FAIL

Rules:

- APIs return JSON, not landing HTML.
- Public endpoints return expected public data.
- Protected endpoints reject unauthenticated users.
- No required route returns 404.
- No route exposes secrets.
- Rate limiting works.
- CORS uses an explicit allowlist.

==================================================
SECTION 6 — LANDING PAGE QA
==================================================

Test the live production page:

https://ivxholding.com

Also test:

https://www.ivxholding.com

Required browsers/viewports:

- Android Chrome
- iPhone Safari viewport
- tablet viewport
- desktop Chrome
- desktop Safari-compatible viewport

Verify every section:

1. Header
2. Hero
3. Properties
4. Deals
5. Reels
6. How It Works
7. Security
8. Reviews
9. Partners
10. Registration
11. Waitlist
12. Investor chat
13. CTA sections
14. Footer
15. Legal links

Verify:

- no page jumping
- no scroll reset
- no white banner
- no black blank section
- no horizontal overflow
- no layout shift
- no broken assets
- no stale bundle
- no console-critical errors
- no failed required network requests
- all CTAs work
- browser back works
- mobile menu works
- www redirects correctly
- canonical URL is correct

==================================================
SECTION 7 — REELS QA
==================================================

Verify the unified reels system.

Required architecture:

- exactly one active player
- maximum three mounted players
- previous, active, next only
- posters for all other items
- stable IDs
- no duplicate audio
- no nested vertical scrolling
- pause on blur
- pause in background
- release distant players
- failed reel does not crash feed
- no expired media URLs

Test:

- play
- pause
- mute
- unmute
- swipe
- replay
- like
- unlike
- comment
- share
- save
- open related property
- return to same reel

Verify native app and landing page use the same reel IDs and engagement totals.

Required stability tests:

- 50 consecutive swipes
- 30-minute feed session
- background and foreground
- slow network
- temporary network loss
- one invalid video source
- one expired URL simulation
- app route leave and return

==================================================
SECTION 8 — REGISTRATION AND MEMBER SYNC
==================================================

Register one controlled QA member through the live landing page.

Verify:

1. Supabase Auth record
2. canonical member record
3. Members module
4. Admin HQ
5. same user ID
6. same email
7. no duplicate record
8. required role saved
9. verification status saved
10. cleanup or QA label applied

Return:

STAGE | RECORD ID | TIMESTAMP | PASS/FAIL | EVIDENCE

Do not expose passwords or private information.

==================================================
SECTION 9 — OWNER LOGIN AND ADMIN HQ
==================================================

Verify the existing owner account.

Test:

- owner login
- invalid password
- non-owner email
- unauthorized route access
- Admin HQ
- Members
- Investors
- CRM
- Variables
- Owner AI
- Chat
- Reels admin
- Landing controls
- Autonomous dashboard
- logout
- session refresh

Protected routes must reject unauthorized users.

Do not ask for credentials unless the real runtime proves the configured credential is missing, expired, revoked, or returns 401/403.

==================================================
SECTION 10 — AUTONOMOUS DASHBOARD QA
==================================================

Audit whether the Autonomous Operations Dashboard exists and works.

Required route:

Owner HQ
→ Autonomous Enterprise
→ Daily Operations

Verify:

- real agent count
- 12-agent view, only if 12 real agents exist
- live activity feed
- tasks numbered 1–100+
- daily report
- code activity
- deployments
- investor activity
- buyer activity
- CRM activity
- owner action center
- notification history
- evidence links
- task status
- trace IDs
- date filters
- category filters
- agent filters
- export functions

Honesty rules:

- No fake agents.
- No simulated activity.
- IDLE if not running.
- UNVERIFIED if no evidence.
- NOT DEPLOYED if committed only.
- NOT VERIFIED if deployed but not tested.

Return:

DASHBOARD ITEM | EXISTS | WORKS | DATA SOURCE | PASS/FAIL | EVIDENCE

==================================================
SECTION 11 — PUSH NOTIFICATION QA
==================================================

Verify notifications for:

- owner action required
- deployment completed
- deployment failed
- critical regression
- device acceptance ready
- daily report ready
- task completed

Test:

- in-app notification
- browser push
- mobile push
- chat notification
- deep link to exact task
- trace ID included
- duplicate prevention
- read/unread status

Do not send secrets in notifications.

==================================================
SECTION 12 — ANDROID QA
==================================================

Use a fresh Android APK from the approved GitHub release.

Verify:

- build ID
- versionCode
- runtimeVersion
- embedded bundle
- no fatal OTA update dependency
- clean install
- offline first launch
- Wi-Fi launch
- cellular launch
- three cold starts
- registration
- owner login
- reels
- chat
- landing sync
- background/foreground
- screen lock/unlock
- 50 reel swipes
- 30-minute session
- no crash
- adb logcat clean of fatal errors

Capture:

- APK URL
- screenshots
- screen recording
- adb log
- build evidence

==================================================
SECTION 13 — IOS QA
==================================================

Verify whether a real iOS app exists.

Return:

- project route
- bundle identifier
- source files
- build status
- signing status
- last build
- TestFlight status
- current functionality

Do not state “iOS native app complete” unless a build exists and has been tested.

If the Swift app is incomplete or unused, mark it accurately.

==================================================
SECTION 14 — SECURITY QA
==================================================

Verify:

- no secrets in repository
- no secrets in frontend bundle
- explicit CORS allowlist
- owner allowlist
- service-role key backend-only
- secure session handling
- protected admin routes
- rate limiting
- input validation
- SQL injection resistance
- XSS protection
- upload validation
- media URL validation
- audit logging
- least-privilege IAM
- CloudFront invalidation permission
- no public admin data

Return all findings by severity:

CRITICAL
HIGH
MEDIUM
LOW

Fix all critical and high issues before certification.

==================================================
SECTION 15 — PERFORMANCE QA
==================================================

Measure:

- TTFB
- first contentful paint
- largest contentful paint
- cumulative layout shift
- landing bundle size
- chat bundle size
- Expo bundle size
- API latency
- reel start time
- reel memory usage
- image optimization
- lazy loading
- cache headers

Separate all applications.

Do not combine landing, chat, and mobile bundle measurements.

==================================================
SECTION 16 — DEPLOYMENT QA
==================================================

Verify:

- GitHub release
- Render backend
- S3 landing page
- CloudFront
- chat frontend
- Supabase migrations
- Expo/EAS build
- Android APK
- iOS build

Return:

COMPONENT | SHA | DEPLOYMENT ID | TIMESTAMP | LIVE URL | PASS/FAIL

Verify CloudFront invalidation and www redirect.

==================================================
SECTION 17 — FINAL EVIDENCE
==================================================

Return one table:

ITEM | EXPECTED | ACTUAL | PASS/FAIL | EVIDENCE

Include at least:

1. Repository owner
2. GitHub SHA
3. Backend SHA
4. Frontend SHA
5. Chat SHA
6. Android build SHA
7. Render deployment
8. S3 deployment
9. CloudFront invalidation
10. www redirect
11. Supabase ownership
12. Expo ownership
13. Rork dependency audit
14. Disaster recovery document
15. API tests
16. Landing QA
17. Reels QA
18. Registration
19. Member sync
20. Owner login
21. Admin HQ
22. Autonomous dashboard
23. Push notifications
24. Android QA
25. iOS status
26. Security
27. Performance
28. Screenshots
29. Recordings
30. Remaining blocker

==================================================
FINAL VERDICT RULE
==================================================

Do not report:

COMPLETE
CERTIFIED
INDEPENDENT
PRODUCTION READY
NO RORK DEPENDENCY

until evidence proves:

- IVX owns all required accounts and code
- production runs without Rork runtime services
- GitHub contains the complete current source
- backend and frontend are aligned
- disaster recovery is documented
- landing page passes
- reels pass
- registration reaches Admin HQ
- owner login passes
- autonomous dashboard is real and visible
- Android passes real-device QA
- critical security issues are zero
- final evidence is attached

If any item remains incomplete, report:

FINAL STATUS = QA IN PROGRESS

FIRST ACTION:

Audit ownership and runtime Rork dependencies before making any new code changes.
